ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a paneer food product entry but no specific entry for honey mustard paneer tikka. The paneer parent node is used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP represents paneer as a cheese product. No specific code exists for honey mustard paneer tikka; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for paneer tikka or honey mustard paneer tikka. SNOMED CT does not cover this specific prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of honey mustard paneer tikka consists of approximately 6 cubes of marinated grilled paneer weighing around 200 g, often served with mint chutney and sliced onions.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Paneer (Cottage Cheese) and Tandoori Preparations
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) provides values for plain paneer and tandoori paneer preparations. Honey mustard paneer tikka is not listed as a discrete entry. Values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer (raw) and tandoori paneer tikka data, with a minor upward adjustment to carbohydrate and sugar to account for the honey and mustard marinade contribution (estimated ~3–4 g carbohydrate per 100 g cooked product). USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy, NDB 01016 — Cheese, cottage, creamed) was used as a cross-reference for paneer macro profile. All adaptations are documented.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for honey mustard paneer tikka. GI is estimated by analogy from paneer (Indian cottage cheese), which has a reported GI of approximately 27 (low GI) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. The honey and mustard marinade contributes a small amount of carbohydrate (~4.8 g sugar per 100 g) but does not materially raise the GI of the overall dish given the dominant protein and fat matrix of paneer. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (27 × 8.4 × 2) / 100 = 4.5, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from marinade spices, salt, and restaurant preparation
hypertension.guidance: A 200 g restaurant serving of honey mustard paneer tikka provides approximately 620 mg sodium, representing about 27% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~27) and high protein content moderating postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Honey mustard paneer tikka has a low GI of approximately 27 and a low glycemic load per serving (~4.5), making it a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-GI accompaniments such as naan or white rice; the honey in the marinade contributes modest free sugars (~4.8 g per 100 g) which should be factored into total carbohydrate intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and dairy-based marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 200 g serving provides approximately 16.4 g saturated fat, which exceeds the 14 g daily saturated fat limit recommended by the American Heart Association for cardiovascular risk reduction; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Honey Mustard Paneer Tikka is an Indian vegetarian tandoor-grilled dish consisting of cubes of fresh paneer (Indian cottage cheese) marinated in a blend of mustard, honey, yogurt, and aromatic spices including turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, and garlic, then skewered and cooked at high heat in a traditional clay tandoor oven. The result is a dish with a lightly charred, smoky exterior, a soft and creamy interior, and a distinctive sweet-tangy-spicy flavour profile from the honey-mustard marinade. Paneer is a fresh acid-set cheese made from full-fat cow or buffalo milk, providing a rich source of calcium (~480 mg per 100 g), high-quality protein (~14.5 g per 100 g), and fat (~14.8 g per 100 g). The dish has a low glycemic index (~27) due to the dominant protein and fat matrix of paneer, making it suitable for individuals managing blood glucose levels. Clinically, the moderate sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g) warrants attention for hypertensive individuals, and the saturated fat content (~8.2 g per 100 g) is relevant for cardiovascular risk management. In Singapore, this dish is commonly served as a starter or appetiser at Indian restaurants and is available on food delivery platforms such as field survey. It is typically accompanied by mint chutney, sliced onions, and lemon wedges. The dish is naturally gluten-free and suitable for lacto-vegetarian and halal diets.
common_user_queries: How many calories in honey mustard paneer tikka?
Is paneer tikka good for diabetics?
What is the GI of paneer tikka?
Is paneer tikka high in protein?
Can I eat paneer tikka if I have high blood pressure?
Is honey mustard paneer tikka gluten free?
What spices are used in paneer tikka marinade?

confidence_reason: Honey mustard paneer tikka is not listed as a discrete entry in ICMR-NIN or any other approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN paneer and tandoori paneer tikka data with a minor carbohydrate adjustment for the honey-mustard marinade. GI is estimated by analogy from paneer GI data in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) paneer and tandoori paneer tikka entries; no discrete entry exists for honey mustard paneer tikka in any approved primary source. 2. Carbohydrate and sugar values include an estimated +3–4 g per 100 g contribution from the honey and mustard marinade, based on typical marinade composition. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for paneer or tandoori preparations. 4. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source for this food category. 5. GI of 27 is an analogous estimate based on paneer (Indian cottage cheese) GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables; no direct GI measurement for honey mustard paneer tikka was found in approved sources. 6. Sodium value reflects a restaurant-prepared product with added salt in the marinade; home-prepared versions may have lower sodium. 7. The dish is served at Bombay Grill and Zaffron Tandoori - Boat Quay on field survey data at SGD 17.00–19.90 per portion.

Estimated GI by analogy from paneer GI data in University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021; documented estimation basis.
